source.title: Efference Copy Is Necessary for the Attenuation of Self-Generated Touch

confidence_rationale: Strong causal design (active vs passive movement dissociates efference copy from tactile contact prediction). Directly supports the single-pass forward-model architecture underlying SD-029: the reference against which reafference is compared is generated from a copy of the actual motor command, not a counterfactual branch. Mapping to nociception is indirect (tactile, not nociceptive), hence the held-back confidence.
failure_signatures: Attenuation was absent during passive movement even though the tactile contact and its timing were equally predictable -- prediction alone is insufficient, a copy of the motor command is required.
Attenuation magnitude in the active condition was modest (~10%, PSE shift ~0.21 N), not binary extinction -- consistent with SD-029's C2 specifying partial rather than full attenuation.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Sensory attenuation of self-generated touch requires an efference copy of the motor command; predictability of tactile input during passive movement is insufficient to produce attenuation.
mapping.ree_translation: The SD-029 comparator residual = z_harm_s_observed - E2_harm_s(z_harm_s_{t-1}, a_actual) relies on the action token a_actual flowing into E2_harm_s. Kilteni 2020 is the cleanest existing test that a copy of the actual motor command -- not merely a prediction of the sensory consequence -- is what drives biological attenuation. This supports SD-029's architectural choice to use E2_harm_s(., a_actual) as the reference, rather than a separate predictive channel.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Modality gap: Kilteni tested self-generated tactile force on the finger. SD-029 operates on a nociceptive reafferent stream z_harm_s. The efference-copy logic is modality-agnostic in principle (PAG/RVM descending modulation has analogous structure) but direct nociceptive-stream evidence is Lalouni 2020 and Lalouni 2025, not Kilteni. Read Kilteni as evidence for the *mechanism* SD-029 instantiates, not for the nociceptive transfer.
mapping.source_context: 40 healthy adults, force-discrimination task on left index finger. Active condition: participant voluntarily pressed the sensor; test tap triggered on opposite finger. Passive condition: finger fell freely onto sensor; identical tactile contact, no motor command. No-movement control: externally-triggered tap.

confidence_components.notes: Peer-reviewed Cell-press journal, pre-registered OSF protocol, Bayesian analysis with null-result quantification (BF=0.20 for passive-vs-control). Mapping_fidelity docked because tactile is not nociceptive. Transfer risk moderate -- efference-copy logic is mechanism-general but the specific PAG/RVM/S1-S2 routing for nociception is not what Kilteni measured.
